name: Cindrel
dexEntry: A palm-sized lizard with ember-orange scales and a permanently smoking snout. Its tail tip glows like a coal and never fully cools. Found basking on warm salt-flat rocks at dawn.

name: Aquill
dexEntry: A roly-poly walrus pup with stubby flippers and a frilled collar that pulses blue when excited. Perpetually damp. Slides more than walks. Locals say finding one means a hot spring is nearby.

name: Brezzet
dexEntry: A tiny fox with ears so large they function as wind scoops, always turned into the breeze. Fur permanently wind-ruffled. Cannot sit still on calm days — it becomes restless and anxious without airflow.

name: Glowick
dexEntry: A fat cave cricket with a bioluminescent abdomen that pulses like a slow heartbeat. Its wide white eyes have no pupils. Completely harmless — drawn to warm light sources, sometimes to its own detriment.

name: Murkling
dexEntry: A small ferret-like creature whose outline never quite resolves — edges blur as if poorly drawn. Only its two faint white eye-dots are reliably visible. Leaves no footprints. Scientists debate whether it has a physical body at all.

name: Peblix
dexEntry: An armadillo pup with a completely smooth grey rock exterior, as if river-tumbled. Tiny curious eyes peer through gaps in its stone-plate face. Rolls into a perfect sphere when frightened — indistinguishable from a large pebble.

name: Humtick
dexEntry: A cicada-beetle the size of a walnut whose wing-cases vibrate visibly when excited, emitting a low hum felt more in the chest than heard by ears. Common near the Resonant Rift's crystal columns. Attracted to harmonic frequencies.

name: Crystub
dexEntry: A chubby bear cub with faceted gem-eyes and small quartz shards sprouting from its back like new teeth — slightly clumsy, always bumping them on things. Refracts nearby light into small rainbow patches on the ground wherever it sits.

name: Cloakrit
dexEntry: A crab whose carapace absorbs all light — not dark-coloured, but genuinely light-consuming. Only its legs are reliably visible in dim conditions. Marine biologists believe it descended from ancient ocean species before the sea dried.

name: Ashpaw
dexEntry: A sooty bear cub with ash-grey fur and ember-glowing pawpads. Leaves faint scorch prints on soft ground. Incredibly affectionate — its hugs are warm in the most literal sense. Beloved by plateau settlements despite the occasional singed carpet.

name: Driftull
dexEntry: A manatee-piglet that lives in the Aureum Basin's hot springs, steam venting constantly from its back and ears. Round, warm, and almost impossibly drowsy. Floats with its eyes closed even when awake.

name: Buzzlit
dexEntry: A bumblebee with an exaggerated round body and wings that beat so fast they're invisible to the naked eye. Emits a low, resonant frequency rather than a buzz — more like a distant foghorn. Surprisingly calming to be around.

name: Galecub
dexEntry: A ferret-weasel with a prehensile windsock tail and enormous nostrils engineered for reading airflow. Always sniffing. Can smell a weather change twelve hours out. Plateau herders keep them as living barometers.

name: Sporik
dexEntry: A stumpy mushroom-toad with stone-textured skin and a cap that releases puffs of mineral spores when stressed. Entirely harmless. Found wherever moisture collects on the salt flats — usually near cracked rock or fossil coral formations.

name: Spectrix
dexEntry: A small deer fawn made entirely of translucent crystal-glass. Its internal structure — what would be organs, bones — is visible but wrong, as if arranged by something that had only heard a description of anatomy. Light bends slightly around it.

name: Flintlet
dexEntry: A small gecko whose scales click against each other like flint-stones when it moves. Sparks occasionally. Entirely unaware this is unusual. Extremely fast and difficult to catch — which is fortunate, as handling one requires heat-resistant gloves.

name: Glintfin
dexEntry: A small manta-pup that glides just above salt-flat puddles. Its iridescent underbelly catches any available light and reflects it upward — on sunny days, a group of Glintfin is visible from the canyon rim as a cluster of moving bright spots.

name: Runekit
dexEntry: A kitten with glowing geometric sigils that drift slowly across its fur like floating script. The patterns have been studied extensively — no two Runekit carry the same sequence, and none have been decoded. Affectionate and entirely inscrutable.

name: Windmite
dexEntry: A pill bug with a small sail-fin that unfurls when it wants to move — it rolls into a ball and uses wind currents to navigate. Completely at the mercy of the breeze when in transit. Somehow always ends up where it intended.

name: Duskrat
dexEntry: A large-eared rat with shadow-shimmer fur that makes it hard to focus on directly — the eye slides off it. Not invisible. Just persistently unnoticed. Population estimates vary wildly because counting them accurately is essentially impossible.

name: Bassolt
dexEntry: A round, grumpy mole with a resonating chest cavity. When it thumps its belly against the ground it sends vibrations through rock for dozens of metres. Used by plateau miners historically to locate hollow ground. Deeply reluctant to cooperate.

name: Glassling
dexEntry: A small, ethereal jellyfish that floats near mineral veins as if tethered to them. Its body is translucent glass with no visible internal structure. Drifts without apparent purpose or destination. Responds to harmonic frequencies by pulsing softly.

name: Tidepug
dexEntry: An aquatic pug-faced creature adapted to hyper-salty water. Its brine-encrusted skin constantly sheds salt crystals — it leaves a faint sparkle trail. Moved to salt-flat puddles when the inland sea receded. Has been adapting, very slowly, ever since.

name: Fluxling
dexEntry: A small kangaroo-mouse surrounded by a heat-shimmer aura that makes it difficult to focus on. The eye keeps adjusting as if it's slightly further away than it is. Warm to the touch. Falls asleep in sun patches and becomes temporarily invisible.

name: Lumenox
dexEntry: A salamander that glows dimly from within, as if lit by a small candle inside its chest. Drawn to warmth. Feels slightly too warm when held. Very affectionate. Very accidentally flammable.

name: Scaldit
dexEntry: A steam-venting newt that lives in geothermal spring shallows. Permanently flushed red. Its skin is heat-hardened enough that it can rest directly on submerged thermal vents. Looks perpetually embarrassed. Is not.

name: Darkspore
dexEntry: A shadowy dandelion-sphere creature that releases clouds of darkness-spores when disturbed — not truly dark, but spores that briefly confuse the eye. Floats low to the ground. Extraordinarily fragile. Somehow abundant.

name: Chuffin
dexEntry: A puffin with a permanently windswept look — feathers always ruffled, expression always mildly surprised. Comically round. Constantly blown off course. Always lands exactly where it intended, by methods that defy aerodynamic analysis. Deeply beloved.

name: Tremlet
dexEntry: A tiny oxen calf with stone-plate horns it has not yet grown into. Trips over them with some regularity. Determined. Gets back up immediately. Always.

name: Omenix
dexEntry: Said to embody the face of the moon that always turns toward the world. One half blazes with cold silver light, the other is pure void. It appears only at the full moon, and its antlers are visible from the horizon.

name: Lunveil
dexEntry: A legendary dragon said to embody the dark side of the moon. Its obsidian armour plates absorb all light, and a single silver eye is all that can be seen in the void of its face. The crescent markings on its chest glow faintly when it stirs.
